Records.description: Two-loop sunrise graph with two equal masses $m_2$ and one different mass $m_1$, in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Elliptic curve geometry. A new function $G$ defined as an integral over the holomorphic period is required for $\varepsilon$-factorisation; $G$ is related to an elliptic integral of the third kind. The GM connection involves $\varpi_0$ and $G$ but not derivatives of periods.
Records.familyName: two-loop sunrise (two equal + one different mass)

Records.description: Two-mass elliptic sunrise with $m_1=m_2=m_b$, $m_3=m_a$. 5 master integrals: two double-tadpoles and three in the highest elliptic sector. Epsilon-factorized differential equations involve complete elliptic integrals of all three kinds ($K, E, \Pi$).
Two-loop sunrise with masses $(m,M,M)$ at pseudothreshold $q^2=-m^2$, arising in NRQCD. $J_{1,2,2}$ evaluated to all orders in $\varepsilon$ via elliptic MPLs and ${}_4F_3$ hypergeometric functions.
Records.familyName: 2-mass 2-loop 2-point sunrise at pseudothreshold in NRQCD (3 propagators)

Records.description: Two-loop sunrise with two distinct masses $(m_1, m_2)$. External momentum $p^2=1$.

Records.authors: Campert, Lijsbeth G.J., Moriello, Francesco, Kotikov, Anatoly
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise integral with two distinct internal masses $(m, M, M)$ at pseudo-threshold kinematics $q^2=-m^2$. Three master integrals $J_{1,1,1}, J_{1,1,2}, J_{1,2,2}$. Finite MI $J_{1,2,2}$ solved to all orders in $\varepsilon$ in terms of elliptic MPLs on the curve $y^2=(x^2-(t-i)/t)(x^2-(t+i)/t)$. Relevant for NRQCD/NRQED applications.

Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Maggio, Sara, Nega, Christoph, Benjamin Sauer, Tancredi, Lorenzo, Wagner, Fabian J.
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise with masses $(m,M,m)$. Canonical DE via MHS method. Maximal cut yields elliptic curve. Three MIs. Primary example for extending $\varepsilon$-factorization to CY.

Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Maggio, Sara, Nega, Christoph, Benjamin Sauer, Tancredi, Lorenzo, Wagner, Fabian J.
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise with propagator masses $(m,M,m)$ in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Maximal cut geometry is an elliptic curve. 3 master integrals. Canonical form via semi-simple/unipotent decomposition of the period matrix. Proceedings example for canonical DEs beyond polylogs.

Records.authors: Ananthanarayan, Balasubramanian, Banik, Sumit, Bernard, Véronique, Friot, Samuel, Ghosh, Shayan, Meißner, Ulf-G.
Records.description: Sunset master integral $H_{1,1,1}(m_1,m_2,m_2;m_3^2)$ with three mass scales in off-shell configuration where two propagator masses are equal but $p^2=m_3^2$ differs from all propagator masses. Full $\varepsilon$-dependent result via two-fold MB representation. Third independent three-mass sunset master integral.

Names: two-loop sunrise (two equal + one different mass)
2-mass 2-loop 2-point sunrise at pseudothreshold in NRQCD (3 propagators)
